objective.description: One sentence objective for this iteration.

self_confidence.description: Planner's confidence that the plan will work (0-1).

evidence_strength.description: How strong the evidence is for the plan's key assumptions (0-1).

reviewer_hint.description: Optional hint for which reviewer/model to use.

next_stage.description: Optional suggested stage update. Forward transitions require review pass; backward transitions are applied immediately.

spec.description: Task spec passed to the orchestrator. IMPORTANT: use exactly these key names. For shell_exec: {"command": "<shell command string>", "timeout": <seconds>}. For codex_exec: {"prompt": "<instruction for Codex>"}. Do NOT use alternative key names like 'script', 'cmd', 'commands', or 'instructions' — they will be rejected.

expected_gain.description: What this approach could have achieved.

risk.description: Key risk or downside of this approach.

reason_rejected.description: Why this approach was not chosen.

alternatives_considered.description: Alternative approaches considered. Required when exploring new directions (iteration 0, stagnation, challenger escalation, stage change, fail streaks).

literature_findings.description: Optional: key literature findings from web search. Each entry should be a citation or finding (e.g., 'Smith et al. (2024) DOI:10.xxx — showed that ...'). Planner should populate this when the objective involves literature survey or research.

should_stop.description: If true, autopilot should stop after this plan.
